user_outcome: A protocol maintainer can run the Baton v0.15 vendor or check workflow without false script-reference matches, unsupported schema-expression failures, or partial primary-worktree writes.
rationale: This is the staged bootstrap boundary: it repairs the existing vendor transaction and exact-schema preflight before S02 is allowed to replace the pinned protocol. The implementation remains local, non-interactive, and linear in mapped source bytes; it handles no PII, credentials, network authentication, UI, or accessibility surface, and must never log source payloads that could contain private adopting-project content.
in_scope: Replace incremental destination writes with a transaction that materialises every mapped byte and compiles every mapped JSON schema before the first primary-worktree mutation, then applies atomic per-file replacements with rollback of original bytes, modes, and non-existence if any apply step fails.

Make check-only mode use the identical materialisation and validation path while prohibiting destination-directory creation, temporary files beneath the repository, pin writes, or other worktree mutation.

Constrain script-reference detection to lexical shell/script tokens so prose such as board.json.shared_touchpoints is accepted while a real scripts/example.sh, example.py, or example.mjs reference still fails closed unless mapped.
Compile the exact v0.15 board-v1 expression with its ECMA-262 negative-lookahead semantics without rewriting normative schema bytes or weakening the forbidden-path cases expressed by that pattern.

Keep work O(total mapped bytes plus mapped file count), preserve source file bytes in diagnostics only as path-safe summaries, and expose deterministic errors naming the failed phase and destination without request bodies, secrets, or source payloads.
out_of_scope: Changing the protocol pin, mapped content set, embedded prompts, embedded schemas, or local Codex and Claude mirrors belongs to S02-v015-parity-and-installs.
Interpreting v0.15 state, board, spec, report, or lifecycle semantics belongs to S03 through S09.
Adding a third-party schema engine or another runtime dependency is prohibited; any such change would require a separate accepted ADR.
Remote fetch authenticity beyond the existing pinned SHA and digest checks is not redesigned in this slice.

effort_complexity.rationale: The failure modes are known and confined to nine existing vendor/compiler touchpoints, with deterministic fault-injection tests and no new product surface.

risks.description: A multi-file apply failure could still leave a mixture of old and new vendored bytes.
risks.mitigation: Fault-inject failure at every apply index and prove byte, mode, and absence equality for every destination before and after the failed transaction.

risks.description: Narrowing script detection could allow a real executable reference through as prose.
risks.mitigation: Table-test bare, relative, absolute-looking, Markdown-code, link-target, punctuation-adjacent, and board.json.shared_touchpoints cases; unknown real script tokens remain blocking.

risks.description: Schema compatibility handling could silently weaken the v0.15 path restriction.
risks.mitigation: Compile from the untouched upstream bytes and mutation-test every accepted and rejected path class named by the exact upstream expression.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the transform scans v0.15 prose containing board.json.shared_touchpoints and fixtures containing real shell, Python, and module-script references THE SYSTEM SHALL accept the prose token and reject every unmapped executable reference with its exact lexical token before any write.

WHEN the validator compiles the exact upstream v0.15 board-v1 bytes THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve those bytes, accept each upstream-valid shared-touchpoint path, and reject dot segments, absolute paths, duplicate regions, and every negative-lookahead near miss.

IF materialisation, schema compilation, destination replacement, or rollback fails at any mapped file THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return non-zero and leave every destination's bytes, mode, and existence equal to the transaction's starting snapshot.

WHEN a maintainer invokes sworn baton vendor --check against the v0.15 checkout THE SYSTEM SHALL exercise the same transform and schema preflight as write mode, report the deterministic diff, exit according to validation success, and create or modify no repository path.
